title: About the General Social Survey | Stats NZ
content:
## About the General Social Survey
10 July 2025, 12:30pm
Information about taking part in the General Social Survey, such as why we carry out the survey, how we selected you, and how to complete the questionnaire.
10 July 2025: Stats NZ will run the General Social Survey in 2026/2027. You can expect to see our interviewers out in the field from July 2026 to June 2027. Details about taking part in the survey will be updated in due course.
The General Social Survey (GSS) is usually carried out every two years (biennially). The survey provides information on the wellbeing of New Zealanders aged 15 years and over. It covers a wide range of social and economic outcomes, and shows how people in different groups within the New Zealand population are faring.
In 2023, the General Social Survey was carried out by Reach Aotearoa (formerly CBG Health Research) on behalf of Stats NZ.

## What you will find on this page
Taking part in the General Social Survey Sample selection Survey completion time Contact us

## Taking part in the General Social Survey
You may have received a letter informing you that you have been chosen for the survey from Reach Aotearoa.
You will need to follow the instructions on the letter to book your interview with Reach Aotearoa. You can contact them by:
- calling 0800 478 783
- emailing info@reach.co.nz
You will be interviewed face-to-face by an interviewer from Reach Aotearoa. If you have been chosen for this survey, you need to answer the questions yourself.
The questions included in this survey are being asked under the Data and Statistics Act 2022.
Our approach to survey compliance has more information about your legal obligation to participate in the General Social Survey.
Approximately 12,000 households in New Zealand are selected to be interviewed for the General Social Survey over the 12month collection period.
A fair statistical method is used to select the households, and every household in the country has a known chance of being selected.
You were selected to be interviewed because you meet the criteria of being 15 years old or older, and live in a selected New Zealand household.
If your household moves away from this address, we will not contact you at your new address. The new occupants of the selected address will complete the survey.

## Survey completion time
The average interview time per household is 45 minutes.
Page updated 10 July 2025
- Wellbeing statistics: 2023 (updated) 25 September 2024
